title: Mutable variables should not be exported
description: Mutable variables should not be exportedAnalyze your codeadaptability-modularmaintainabilityreliabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIn JavaScript, a mutable variable is one whose value can be changed after it has been initially set. This is in contrast to immutable variables, whose values cannot be changed once they are set.Exporting mutable variables can lead to unpredictable behavior and bugs in your code. This is because any module that imports the variable can change its value.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Consider using 'const' instead of 'let' or 'var' for immutable variables
